seoTitle: Build a Social Media Engagement Dashboard in Power BI
metaDescription: Learn how to build a social media dashboard in Power BI, from importing engagement data to visualizing KPIs marketers actually track.
intro: A social media dashboard turns scattered engagement numbers into a single view your marketing team can act on. This guide walks through importing your social data, choosing the right visuals for engagement metrics, and setting up the dashboard so it stays useful over time. It's built for marketers and analysts who want a repeatable process rather than a one-off report.

steps.heading: Set up your data source
steps.body: Bring your social media data into Power BI, whether that's exported metrics or a connected data source, and make sure the fields are clean before building visuals.

steps.heading: Create visuals for engagement analytics
steps.body: Build charts that track interactions like likes, shares, comments, and reach, choosing visual types that make trends easy to spot at a glance.

steps.heading: Analyze key performance indicators
steps.body: Add KPI cards or summary visuals that surface the numbers your team checks most often, such as engagement rate or follower growth.

steps.heading: Automate reports and insights
steps.body: Set up scheduled refreshes so the dashboard updates automatically, cutting down on manual reporting work for your team.

faq.question: What social media metrics work best in a Power BI dashboard?
faq.answer: Engagement metrics like likes, shares, comments, reach, and follower growth translate well into visuals that make trends and campaign performance easy to track.

faq.question: Do I need a live connection to social platforms for this dashboard?
faq.answer: Not necessarily — you can start with exported data and move to an automated connection later once you know which metrics matter most to your team.

faq.question: How often should the dashboard refresh?
faq.answer: It depends on how frequently you post and review performance, but many marketing teams refresh daily or weekly to match their campaign cadence.

faq.question: Can this dashboard track multiple social platforms at once?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as the data from each platform is structured consistently, you can combine sources into a single unified engagement view.
